places.name: Cafebrería El Péndulo
places.description: Part bookstore, part café, and part forest.

places.name: La Casa Azul
places.description: Frida Kahlo's childhood home, now a museum of her life and works.

places.name: Biblioteca Vasconcelos
places.description: A jaw-dropping "megalibrary" that turns book-browsing into a geometric adventure.

places.name: Mercado de Sonora
places.description: Superstitious? Discover the witchy side of Mexico through its largest esoteric market.

places.name: Soumaya Museum
places.description: 66,000 pieces of art, donated by one of the world's richest men.

places.name: Diego Rivera and Frida Kahlo House-Studio Museum
places.description: The famous artist couple lived and worked here, in two different houses connected by a bridge.

places.name: Desierto de los Leones (Desert of the Lions)
places.description: Explore the atmospheric ruins of an abandoned convent in the dense forests of Mexico's first national park.

places.name: Minichelista Restaurant
places.description: A quirky and charming restaurant founded by artists.

places.name: The Sweets Section at Mercado de la Merced
places.description: Mexico City's largest market contains a Willy Wonka-esque wonderland.
